svg.text: Private Send Flow (what happens in code)
1) Wallet chooses spend source
If enough confirmed private notes exist, spend private -> private directly.
If not enough private notes, wallet can shield first: public -> private-self, then private -> recipient.
2) Fee and amount checks happen before broadcast
fee_atoms = max(vsize * fee_per_vb_atoms, min_tx_fee_atoms)
No note can spend more than its amount. No negative outputs. No fake amount creation.
3A) Private input proof material
- nullifier (one-time spend marker)
- anchor + merkle path (prove note is in note tree)
- note signature (prove ownership of note key)
Mempool and consensus both reject duplicate nullifiers and invalid proofs.
3B) Private output creation
- fresh output note pubkey
- encrypted payload for receiver bundle
- commitment binds note key + amount + nonce
Outputs are hidden as commitments; chain does not expose raw private amounts.
4) Consensus validation + state update
- reject double-spend/nullifier reuse
- reject malformed private fields or ownership mismatch
- if valid: mark nullifier spent, insert new notes, enforce confirmations for spendability
Wallet UI should show pending first, then spendable only after required confirmations.
